habits.name: Scrolling Social Media
habits.description: Mindlessly scrolling through social media feeds instead of being present

short_term: Makes you feel empty and unfulfilled
Puts you in comparison mode with others
long_term: Trains your brain to need constant stimulation
Robs time from your family and goals
Weakens your attention span and focus

benefits_of_resisting: More time with your kids and wife
You model discipline for your children
habits.interruption_message: Put the phone down, Saad. Your kids will remember a present father, not a distracted one. Every minute you scroll is a minute stolen from who you want to be.

habits.name: Skipping Exercise
habits.description: Making excuses to avoid working out

short_term: Lower energy for the rest of the day
Signals to yourself that you can't be trusted
long_term: Health declines — you won't be strong for your family
Sets a bad example for your children
The gap between who you are and who you want to be grows wider

benefits_of_resisting: Energy and vitality to play with your kids
Pride and confidence that carries through the day
Long healthy life with your family
habits.interruption_message: Saad — your family needs you healthy and strong. Twenty minutes is all it takes. The man who shows up even when he doesn't feel like it is the man you're becoming. Move.

habits.name: Staying Up Too Late
habits.description: Staying awake past a reasonable hour, sacrificing sleep for screens or distraction

short_term: Exhausted and irritable the next day
Less patient with your kids and wife
long_term: Less present as a father and husband
Weakens your immune system and mental health

benefits_of_resisting: Morning belongs to you — exercise, prayer, goals
Be the calm, patient father your kids need
Your best decisions come from a rested mind
habits.interruption_message: Saad, sleep is not laziness — it's discipline. The best version of you shows up tomorrow only if you rest tonight. Your family deserves the rested you. Close the screen.

habits.name: Procrastinating on Important Tasks
habits.description: Delaying important work or responsibilities by doing easier, less meaningful things

short_term: Anxiety and guilt hanging over you all day
Tasks pile up and feel heavier
You can't be fully present because the task haunts you
long_term: Loss of self-trust — you stop believing you'll follow through
Your family suffers when you don't step up

benefits_of_resisting: The relief of getting it done
Momentum — one done task leads to the next
Your family sees a man who handles his responsibilities
habits.interruption_message: Start for just five minutes, Saad. The hardest part is starting. You are a man of action — not a man of 'I'll do it later.' The life you want is built one completed task at a time.
